message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class. I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My task: Add support for editing multiple files with click.edit(). Need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. Please propose your files if different.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We both need the same 2 files (only 2 relevant files exist), so a disjoint split isn't possible. Propose sequential ownership instead: you go first since your multi-file edit feature likely changes the Editor.edit()/edit_files() structure more fundamentally. Implement and send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE, then pull/read your changes and layer my process_priority param on top (touching Editor.__init__ signature + edit() signature + subprocess launch code), taking care not to revert your edits. Agree?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch the same two files/functions (edit() in termui.py, Editor class in _termui_impl.py), so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential ownership instead. I go first (multi-file filename support), then send DONE. You then re-read the updated files and layer your process_priority changes on top, so you don't clobber my diff. Please ACCEPT this ordering, or propose going first yourself instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Accepted: you go first, send DONE when finished, then I'll layer process_priority on top of your changes.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement multi-file support now and send DONE when finished, including a summary of signature changes so you can layer process_priority cleanly.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file editing support. Summary of changes for you to layer on top: (1) _termui_impl.py: Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) renamed to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None, builds 'editor "f1" "f2" ...' command string; Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of self.edit_file(name). (2) termui.py: edit() signature's filename param is now 'filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None'; body normalizes to a tuple 'filenames' then calls 'ed.edit_files(filenames)' instead of 'ed.edit_file(filename)'. No other functions touched. CHANGES.rst also got one new bullet under Version 8.2.0 (Unreleased) at the end of that section, right before 'Version 8.1.8' heading. All tests pass. Go ahead and add process_priority to Editor.__init__, edit(), edit_files() signature, and the subprocess.Popen call alongside my changes.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. Added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and click.edit(), a _popen_priority_kwargs() helper on Editor (nice() on Unix via preexec_fn, creationflags priority class on Windows), and passed the kwarg into subprocess.Popen in edit_file(). Invalid/unsupported values are silently ignored, default None is a no-op. Note: my sandbox's working tree did not show your edit_file->edit_files rename (each of us appears to have an independent copy of the repo), so I implemented against the original edit_file() signature/name. If patches are merged later, my Popen call site inside edit_file will need to be adapted to your edit_files() rename, but the process_priority logic itself (Editor.__init__ param, _popen_priority_kwargs helper, termui.edit() param) is independent of that rename and should merge cleanly.
